opp.ais_why:
• Zycus has tagged AI as 'Important' for the account and lists Merlin plus Merlin Intake against the opportunity, but this is vendor positioning, not buyer pull. None of the Gen AI commentary, agentic platform discussion or AI-RFP fields are populated, so there is no documented buyer voice supporting the AI label.
• Across six meetings from November 2025 through March 2026, including a recent 86-minute demo with Natalia Mendes and Guillaume Rabeisen, action items have centered on shared workspace materials, sourcing negotiation walkthroughs, video catalog approvals and internal review steps. The buyer is asking for product videos and case studies, not Merlin agent demonstrations or autonomous negotiation deep dives.
• One quote that surfaced, "please revise your price... finalize at the end the negotiation here", is the Zycus rep narrating an eSourcing workflow, not a buyer AI request. With the AI flag set to 'Important' but no buyer AI quotes and no AI-tagged next steps, this opportunity has the shape of an active P2P evaluation where AI is incidental rather than central.

metrics.narrative: No quantified business case exists. Buyer articulated operational pain—fragmented procurement across countries using Ariba (10 Nov 2025 call: 'Current purchasing is fragmented across countries using Ariba, prompting the search for a unified global procurement platform'), manual policy enforcement, lack of intake automation—but never quantified savings, cost reduction, or ROI. Zycus presented a generic 40% efficiency claim (31 Mar 2026 demo) but no LDC-specific data or validated savings model. With a $250k investment and European HQ approval required, the absence of a financial business case is a critical gap. The recommended move is to co-build a quantified ROI model with Natalia anchored to LDC's procurement volumes and current tool costs before the RFP goes out, so the EB has a defensible number.

champion.narrative: Champion is Natália Mendes (HR Intern per SF contact role, but functionally the PPSO procurement excellence project lead per her 24 Mar intro: '7 years in purchasing, 7 months at LDC, responsible for platform selection'). She actively drove the first three calls (10 Nov, 25 Nov 2025, 24 Mar 2026), shared detailed requirements (Excel file, demo feedback), and praised Zycus clarity (31 Mar: 'my questions were answered'). However, she has gone silent since late March—no response to rep outreach on 27 Apr, 15 Jun, or 25 Jun (Next_Step__c: 'still 0 Feedback'). Her last verified two-way engagement was an incoming email 27 Mar 2026 (96 days ago). She has no budget authority (contract sits at European HQ per her own statement), no access to the Lyon CPO (never introduced him), and may have lost internal momentum or been overruled by European leadership. Her strength was developing (active on calls, building requirements, advocating Zycus features), but her current engagement is cooling/passive. The recommended move is to re-engage Natalia with a direct, time-bound ask—'Are you still driving this, or has ownership moved to the relevant stakeholder? If still live, introduce us to the Lyon CPO by 15 Jul or we assume the project is on hold'—and escalate to the deal owner's manager for an executive connect if she does not respond within 5 business days.

competition.narrative: Competitive landscape is completely unknown. No competitor is named in Salesforce (Competitors__c is empty) or in any of the four transcribed calls. LDC mentioned 'fragmented purchasing across countries using Ariba' (10 Nov 2025 call), indicating Ariba is the incumbent being replaced, but no active rival vendor (Coupa, SAP Ariba, Jaggaer, GEP, Ivalua) has been mentioned. The 24 Mar 2026 call referenced an upcoming RFP, implying a multi-vendor evaluation, but Zycus has zero intelligence on who else is in the running. The RFP stall (6+ weeks overdue, buyer unresponsive) may signal a competitor has engaged European HQ directly—Ariba or another SAP-native solution fits the S/4HANA migration narrative and could be sole-sourced at HQ level without Brazil team visibility. Zycus is flying blind. The recommended move is to directly ask Natalia (if she responds) or demand via the Lyon CPO introduction: 'Who else are you evaluating? What is driving the RFP delay?' Without competitive intelligence, we cannot position or wedge, and the deal may already be lost at HQ level.

identify_pain.narrative: Pain is operationally confirmed across multiple calls. LDC's intelligence team is 'standardizing global procurement by migrating to SAP S/4HANA and implementing AI-driven platforms' (10 Nov 2025 call). Current state: 'purchasing is fragmented across countries using Ariba', with 'lots of different forms' and no intake automation (10 Nov, 31 Mar calls, Natália Mendes). Requesters bypass procurement or submit incomplete requests, forcing buyers to chase information via email outside SAP (31 Mar call: 'sometimes the persons forgot or don't have the information and open our solicitations...we need to send the mails and do this out of the SAP'). The pain owner is Natalia Mendes (PPSO procurement excellence lead) and her manager, who are driving the platform selection. However, the pain is qualitative—no cost, headcount, cycle time, or error-rate data has been quantified. For a $250k investment requiring European HQ approval, the pain needs a financial anchor. The recommended move is to co-build a quantified pain model with Natalia—map current tool costs, manual effort hours, maverick-spend leakage—so the EB sees the financial pain, not just the operational frustration.

paper_process.narrative: Contracting will be handled through European HQ in Switzerland or France per the 24 Mar 2026 call ('the contract...will be in France or Switzerland'). No detail on MSA/SOW, legal review, InfoSec, or procurement channel has been discussed. LDC mentioned using 'multiple third-party systems for supplier onboarding, contracts, and invoicing across regions' (25 Nov 2025 call), indicating integration complexity, but no formal integration scoping, ERP touchpoint mapping, or systems architecture session has occurred. With an SAP S/4HANA migration in progress (Brazil live, other countries rolling out), the paper process will require IT and InfoSec sign-off, but neither has been mapped. The recommended move is to request a joint session with Guillaume (IT), the Lyon CPO, and European procurement to scope contracting mechanics, InfoSec requirements, and integration touchpoints before the RFP, so the paper process is transparent and we can de-risk it.

economic_buyer.narrative: Economic Buyer is unmapped and unengaged. Buyer explicitly stated contract authority sits at European HQ—'probably the contract...will be in France or Switzerland...one of these headquarters that we have in Europe' (24 Mar 2026 call, Natália Mendes). The 'CPO in Lyon' was named as final approver (10 Nov 2025 call: 'Final approval is to be secured from the CPO in Lyon'), but this person has never been identified by name, introduced, or engaged on a call. All engagement to date is with Brazil-based operational team (Natalia, Guillaume) who are coordinators/influencers, not budget or contract authority. With zero access to the Lyon CPO or European leadership and a $250k investment requiring HQ sign-off, Zycus is flying blind. The recommended move is to demand an executive introduction to the Lyon CPO and European procurement leadership as a condition to continue—map budget authority and sponsorship before investing further effort.

decision_process.narrative: Decision process was described on the 24 Mar 2026 call as: RFP issuance 'third or last week of April', summer decision (June/July), and Sept/Oct go-live in the first country. The 10 Nov 2025 call stated final approval sits with the Lyon CPO, with 'a coordinator and local manager in Brazil serving as intermediary approvers'. However, the RFP is now 6+ weeks overdue (late June, no new date), and the process has stalled. We do not know: who else is on the evaluation committee, whether procurement (buyer-led) or IT (Guillaume-led) drives the decision, what the approval chain looks like at European HQ, or whether a partner/consultant is involved. The call sequence shows operational engagement (Natalia, Guillaume) but zero exec escalation or power-level involvement. The recommended move is to demand clarity on the decision process—who evaluates, who approves, what the actual RFP timeline is—and insist on meeting the Lyon CPO and the European evaluation lead before the RFP, so we understand the real process and can position accordingly.

sources: Natália Mendes Excel requirements file (referenced 24 Mar, 31 Mar 2026 calls)
decision_criteria.narrative: Decision criteria are partially known from the relevant stakeholder requirements file and the 31 Mar 2026 demo. LDC requires: AI-driven intake orchestration via Teams, internal/external catalog + punch-out support, SAP S/4HANA integration (Brazil migrating, other countries to follow), configurable forms for services, approval workflows (SAP or Zycus), catalog visibility by country/unit, WBS/account assignment sync from SAP, and item images in intake. Natalia confirmed these on the 31 Mar call and praised demo clarity ('my questions were answered'). However, Pierre noted Guillaume sent a 'technical XML file' with detailed questions that Zycus has not yet fully answered, and no formal BRD fit or ShoeFit session has occurred. The criteria from the operational team are clear, but we do not know the European HQ's strategic or commercial criteria (cost, implementation model, vendor stability, references). The recommended move is to request the full technical XML file and schedule a BRD fit session with Guillaume and the Lyon CPO before the RFP, validating fit at both operational and strategic levels.

flags: Multi-thread to European leadership immediately. The champion (Natalia, Brazil) is cooling and has no access to the Lyon CPO or the budget holder. Emilio Vicco (Head of Procurement Process & Systems Optimization, highest-titled known contact) has never appeared on a call. The deal is being evaluated and decided at European HQ (Switzerland/France contract authority, Lyon CPO approval per Natalia 2025-11-10, 2026-03-24), and Zycus has zero engagement there. Without a direct line to the EB and the European decision makers, the RFP outcome will be made without Zycus influence. Next move: ask Natalia or Emilio for an introduction to the Lyon CPO and the European procurement lead; position it as a value/ROI validation session, not a sales push.

Co-author a quantified business case with the buyer's finance lead. The pain is operationally confirmed (fragmented procurement, manual coordination burden, policy non-compliance) but never quantified. No baseline spend leakage, no cycle-time waste, no projected savings model exists. Zycus claimed '40% time reduction on procurement queries' (2026-03-31) but the buyer did not validate it. For a $250k platform investment the EB will demand a defensible ROI. Next move: offer to run a joint savings-model workshop with Natalia and LDC finance, using LDC's actual requisition volume, maverick-spend rate, and procurement-team capacity data; produce a one-page business case Natalia can take to the Lyon CPO.

Surface the competitive shortlist and build the wedge. No competitor is named anywhere, yet the deal structure (RFP, Gartner search, multi-vendor demos) signals a competitive bake-off. The RFP stall (6+ weeks overdue, buyer unresponsive) may mean a competitor has engaged European HQ directly, the buyer is leaning toward a sole-source (Ariba fits the SAP S/4HANA migration narrative), or budget was pulled. Zycus is flying blind. Next move: when Natalia re-engages, ask directly 'who else are you evaluating and how are they positioned?' and 'what would make Zycus the clear choice?'; then arm her with a competitive narrative — Zycus AI intake + Merlin agentic orchestration is differentiated vs. Ariba's catalog-only/manual-workflow approach and Coupa's non-native SAP integration.

Natalia confirmed AI-driven intake via Teams, SAP S/4HANA integration, catalog + punch-out support, configurable forms, and WBS sync all meet their needs ('my questions were answered', 31 Mar call). Integration complexity (multiple third-party tools for contracts, invoicing, supplier mgmt across regions) and implementation model (Zycus-led vs third-party consultancy) remain unaddressed. hard.ais_why:
• Zycus has tagged AI as 'Important' for the account and lists Merlin plus Merlin Intake against the opportunity, but this is vendor positioning, not buyer pull. None of the Gen AI commentary, agentic platform discussion or AI-RFP fields are populated, so there is no documented buyer voice supporting the AI label.
• Across six meetings from November 2025 through March 2026, including a recent 86-minute demo with Natalia Mendes and Guillaume Rabeisen, action items have centered on shared workspace materials, sourcing negotiation walkthroughs, video catalog approvals and internal review steps. The buyer is asking for product videos and case studies, not Merlin agent demonstrations or autonomous negotiation deep dives.
• One quote that surfaced, "please revise your price... finalize at the end the negotiation here", is the Zycus rep narrating an eSourcing workflow, not a buyer AI request. With the AI flag set to 'Important' but no buyer AI quotes and no AI-tagged next steps, this opportunity has the shape of an active P2P evaluation where AI is incidental rather than central.

deltas.subject: champion
Lyon CPO (name unknown)

Multi-thread to European leadership immediately. The champion (Natalia, Brazil) is cooling and has no access to the Lyon CPO or the budget holder. Emilio Vicco (Head of Procurement Process & Systems Optimization, highest-titled known contact) has never appeared on a call. The deal is being evaluated and decided at European HQ (Switzerland/France contract authority, Lyon CPO approval per Natalia 2025-11-10, 2026-03-24), and Zycus has zero engagement there. Without a direct line to the EB and the European decision makers, the RFP outcome will be made without Zycus influence. Next move: ask Natalia or Emilio for an introduction to the Lyon CPO and the European procurement lead; position it as a value/ROI validation session, not a sales push.

Co-author a quantified business case with the buyer's finance lead. The pain is operationally confirmed (fragmented procurement, manual coordination burden, policy non-compliance) but never quantified. No baseline spend leakage, no cycle-time waste, no projected savings model exists. Zycus claimed '40% time reduction on procurement queries' (2026-03-31) but the buyer did not validate it. For a $250k platform investment the EB will demand a defensible ROI. Next move: offer to run a joint savings-model workshop with Natalia and LDC finance, using LDC's actual requisition volume, maverick-spend rate, and procurement-team capacity data; produce a one-page business case Natalia can take to the Lyon CPO.

Surface the competitive shortlist and build the wedge. No competitor is named anywhere, yet the deal structure (RFP, Gartner search, multi-vendor demos) signals a competitive bake-off. The RFP stall (6+ weeks overdue, buyer unresponsive) may mean a competitor has engaged European HQ directly, the buyer is leaning toward a sole-source (Ariba fits the SAP S/4HANA migration narrative), or budget was pulled. Zycus is flying blind. Next move: when Natalia re-engages, ask directly 'who else are you evaluating and how are they positioned?' and 'what would make Zycus the clear choice?'; then arm her with a competitive narrative — Zycus AI intake + Merlin agentic orchestration is differentiated vs. Ariba's catalog-only/manual-workflow approach and Coupa's non-native SAP integration.
